title: Life and Death in the Fast Lane: Police Enforcement and Traffic Fatalities
notes: DeAngelo & Hansen (2014), AEJ: Economic Policy. Oregon State Police mass layoff Feb 1, 2003 (single-timing DiD). State-level monthly panel, 47 states (excl AK, HI, VT), 2000-2005. Treatment: Oregon (fips=41) from Feb 2003 (mn_yr=38); Jan 2003 coded as pre-treatment since layoff was Feb 1. Outcome: fat_vmt = traffic fatalities per VMT (all highways, all weather conditions). OUTCOME SELECTION: Protocol rule (ii) largest N selects full US sample (N=3384, Table 4 col 1) over geographic control WA+ID (N=216, col 2). Rule (iii.a) selects fat_vmt (aggregate) over fat_good_vmt (outside city/dry weather subcategory). Original beta_twfe=0.7103 is the raw coefficient; paper reports semi-elasticity=0.05 (=0.7103/13.00 mean). DATA FILE: original fatal_analysis_file_2014.dta is MISSING from replication package. Using synth_file_2014.dta which contains identical variables for all 47 states; treatment variable is already coded in the data. This file has 47 states (missing VT fips=50) vs paper's 48 (N=3456 vs 3384, difference of 1 state). Stata: areg fat_vmt treatment mon_* y_* precip temp un_rate max_speed, a(fips) robust cluster(state). FE structure: fips absorbed by areg, year+month dummies as covariates = equivalent to feols(... | fips + year + month). Clustering: state-level for full US sample (fips is 1:1 with state). Paper also reports WA+ID geographic control (robust SE, no clustering) and synthetic control (5 states with weights). CS-DID note: only 1 treated unit (Oregon) with 46 never-treated states; att_gt should work but variance estimation may be imprecise with single treated unit. SA trivial for single timing but included for consistency. Event study: monthly data with 37 pre-periods and 35 post-periods relative to Feb 2003; event_pre/post set to 6 for readable plot (6 months each side). No run_csdid_nyt because there are never-treated states available (run_csdid_nt instead). No Bacon decomposition because single timing (no heterogeneous treatment timing). | AUDIT 2026-04-19: Paper-auditor WITHIN_TOLERANCE (1.45% — back-calculation of raw β from paper's 2-decimal semi-elasticity 0.05). TWFE 0.70 matches paper. Spec A ANOMALY: CS-NT with 4 twfe_controls AMPLIFIES 148% to 1.73 — Pattern 51-style inflation in single-treated-unit design (only Oregon treated vs 46 never-treated; propensity-score weighting distorts with K=1 treated). Spec B (no ctrls) CS-NT=0.70 matches TWFE byte-identical. Use Spec B (=0.70) as headline CS representation; Spec A (=1.73) is artefact of K=1 treated + DR overfit. Pre-trends 3/5 significant in CS-NT (design signal); HonestDiD D-FRAGILE avg/peak.
